content:
I am a postdoctoral fellow at KAUST, working on Optimization for Machine Learning in the group supervised by Prof. Peter Richtárik. Previously, I worked with Prof. Diogo Gomes on Computer Algebra for Differential Equations.
I did my PhD in Mathematics at Yerevan State University, Armenia under the supervision of Prof. Grigori Karagulyan.

I am a postdoctoral fellow at KAUST, working on Optimization for Machine Learning in the group supervised by Prof. Peter Richtárik. Previously, I worked with Prof. Diogo Gomes on Computer Algebra for Differential Equations.

title: Mher Safaryan

summary: Various gradient compression schemes have been proposed to mitigate the communication cost in distributed training of large scale machine learning models. Sign-based methods, such as signSGD, have recently been gaining popularity because of their simple compression rule and connection to adaptive gradient methods, like ADAM. In this paper, we perform a general analysis of sign-based methods for non-convex optimization. Our analysis is built on intuitive bounds on success probabilities and does not rely on special noise distributions nor on the boundedness of the variance of stochastic gradients. Extending the theory to distributed setting within a parameter server framework, we assure variance reduction with respect to number of nodes, maintaining 1-bit compression in both directions and using small mini-batch sizes. We validate our theoretical findings experimentally.
title: On Stochastic Sign Descent Methods

summary: We prove Fatou type theorem on almost everywhere convergence of convolution integrals in spaces Lp(1
title: On Generalizations of Fatou's Theorem in $L^p$ for Convolution Integrals with General Kernels

summary: The paper considers differentiation properties of density bases formed of bounded open sets.We prove that two quasi-equivalent subbases of some density basis differentiate the same class of non-negative functions. Applications for bases formed of rectangles are discussed.
title: On an Equivalency of Rare Differentiation Bases of Rectangles

summary: The paper considers differentiation properties of rare bases of dyadic rectangles corresponding to increasing sequences { u_k} of integers. We prove that the condition sup_k ( u_{k+1}− u_k)
title: On an equivalence for differentiation bases of dyadic rectangles

summary: In this paper, the concept of a g-dimonoid is introduced and the construction of a free g-dimonoid is described.(A g-dimonoid is a duplex satisfying two additional identities.)
title: Construction of free g-dimonoids

summary: In 1927 Littlewood constructed an example of bounded holomorphic function on the unit disk, which diverges almost everywhere along rotated copies of any given curve in the unit disk ending tangentially to the boundary. This theorem was the complement of a positive theorem of Fatou 1906, establishing almost everywhere nontangential convergence of bounded holomorphic functions. There are several generalizations of the Littlewood's theorem which proofs are based on the specific properties of Poisson kernel. We generalize Littlewood's theorem for operators having general kernels.
title: On a theorem of Littlewood
On generalizations of Fatou’s theorem for the integrals with general kernels
